preview_text:
The model favors Brazil at 63%, with Scotland's win probability at 12%. Brazil enters as a heavy favorite, leaving the underdog with roughly one-in-eight odds of victory on a neutral surface.

The draw probability of 25% from the model suggests competitive balance in the tail scenarios, yet Brazil's advantage across the W/D/L spread is substantial. A Scotland win remains plausible but unlikely; draws and Brazil victories dominate the outcome space, indicating Brazil is expected to control much of the match.

The market consensus edges Brazil higher at 69%, roughly 5 percentage points above the model, while assigning draws lower at 19%. Polymarket prices align closely with the sportsbook view. The divergence reflects differing assumptions about Brazil's edge, though all three sources rank outcomes identically and treat Scotland as a clear underdog.
